blocks.text: slept fine. That surprised him a little.
held the door for a second. Didn't examine it. Let it close.
was still standing at the water when he looked back. She didn't turn around.
stood there a moment longer than he needed to. Then went in.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: You're up early.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Couldn't sleep. I come out here when that happens — something about the water before anyone else is on it. You too?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Usually up at six. Habit.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Good habit.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Cold out here. Still worth it.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: How long have you been out?

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Since four-thirty.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: That's not insomnia. That's something else.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Ha. Yeah. Maybe.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: You ever feel like you're carrying something and you're not entirely sure when you picked it up, or how heavy it actually is?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: It's like that. Anyway.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I was actually thinking — last night, I said there was something I needed to get to Dean. I should find it today. Before the weekend's over.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: What is it?

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Something I was supposed to give him a while ago and I just — I kept forgetting. That's the honest version. I kept putting it down and walking away from it. I'm going to find it today.
He said it like he wanted to be held to it. Like saying it out loud was an intention he was making real.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: The light's different out here in the morning. Worth getting up for even when the rest of it doesn't cooperate.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: How long have you known Cade?

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Twenty-three years. Longer than anyone here. Why?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: You know him well enough to know when something's wrong.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Yeah. I do. He's — Cade's dealing with something. He's been dealing with it for a few months and he doesn't want to talk about it yet. I'm waiting him out.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Is he okay?

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: He will be. That's my read. I've seen Cade in bad shape before and this isn't that. He just needs time.

blocks.prompt: Marcus says he's waiting Cade out. What does Julian make of that?

options.label: He means it. He's the kind of person who waits people out and means it.
Cade is carrying something Marcus doesn't fully know yet.
Julian believes him. Marcus knows his people. That matters.
